title: daucus griffithii
intro: Daucus griffithii, also known as the wild carrot or Gajar, is a plant species belonging to the family Apiaceae. It is a perennial herb with a long history of use in traditional medicine, particularly in the Ayurvedic and Unani systems, where it is valued for its medicinal properties. The plant is believed to have anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and diuretic properties, making it useful in treating various health conditions, including fever, digestive issues, and urinary tract infections. Daucus griffithii can be prepared as a herbal tea, infusion, or decoction, and is also used in the form of essential oil, which is said to have a soothing and calming effect on the body.
